steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two sentences. Sentence 1: above the Kármán line, lift no longer keeps an aircraft aloft. Sentence 2: the aircraft sustains flight by velocity instead. The second sentence introduces a replacement mechanism for the one that failed — not an example, not a concession, not something that happens regardless.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Regardless,': Signals that the outcome is the same despite some obstacle. Here the outcome (sustaining flight) uses a completely different mechanism, so the relationship is replacement, not indifference. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For instance,': Introduces an example of something already stated. The second sentence is not an example of lift failing — it describes what happens instead. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Granted,': Concedes a point to strengthen a counterargument. No counterargument is being built here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Instead,': Signals direct replacement: lift is gone, so velocity takes over. This matches the structure of the passage precisely. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
